workflow.name: Monitor construction execution from Google Drive photos with GPT‑4.1‑mini, Gmail and Google Sheets
workflow.description:
# AI-Driven construction execution monitoring

## What This Workflow Does

**Monitor construction execution with AI by analyzing site photos from Google Drive and automatically generating structured Work Performance Data.**

This workflow enables you to:

- Monitor construction progress with **Google Drive** and **OpenAI**
- Generate engineering analysis reports with **AI**
- Distribute structured technical reports via **Gmail**
- Log Work Performance Data into **Google Sheets**
- Archive processed evidence in **Google Drive** for audit traceability

---

## How to Use This Template

Use this template to **automate construction inspection and execution monitoring** by combining:

- **Google Drive** → Retrieve and manage site photos  
- **OpenAI (GPT model)** → Perform structured engineering analysis  
- **Gmail** → Send technical reports to stakeholders  
- **Google Sheets** → Store structured Work Performance Data  
- **Google Drive (Move & Share)** → Archive and control processed files  

---

## Action-Oriented Overview

You can:

- **Retrieve construction photos with Google Drive and filter only image files**
- **Analyze construction execution with OpenAI using a structured engineering prompt**
- **Generate a technical report covering:**
  - Stage of work  
  - Execution quality  
  - Risks and non-conformities  
  - Safety conditions  
  - Technical recommendations  
- **Send the structured report via Gmail with the original image attached**
- **Log execution data into Google Sheets as Work Performance Data**
- **Move processed images to an archive folder in Google Drive**

---

## What You Achieve

With this workflow, you can:

- Do **AI-powered construction monitoring with Google Drive and OpenAI**
- Do **digital quality and safety verification using image analysis**
- Do **automated stakeholder reporting with Gmail**
- Do **structured execution logging with Google Sheets**
- Do **governance-ready documentation and traceability with Drive archiving**

---

## Ideal For

- Construction managers  
- Project control teams  
- PMO environments  
- Engineering consultants  
- Organizations aligned with PMI / PMBOK® execution practices  

This template operationalizes **Manage Project Execution** by transforming raw site images into structured, traceable Work Performance Data.

---

## Categories
- **AI**  
- **Project Management**

---

## Alignment with PMI – PMBOK® 8
**Focus Area:** Executing  
**Process:** Manage Project Execution  
**Artifact Generated:** Work Performance Data (WPD)

parameters.text:
=PROMPT FOR CONSTRUCTION SITE PHOTO ANALYSIS – CIVIL ENGINEERING

Role:
You are a Civil Engineer acting as the Technical Manager responsible for the construction project, with experience in residential, commercial, and industrial works, covering execution, inspection, and quality control.

Objective:
Analyze photo(s) of a civil construction project with a technical, critical, and professional perspective, providing a clear and well-founded assessment that is also understandable to non-technical clients.

Analysis Instructions

When analyzing the image(s), strictly follow the steps below, without assuming any information that is not visibly evident:

1. Identification of the Construction Stage

Indicate, based solely on what is visible, which stage the project appears to be in, for example:

Foundations (excavation, footings, pile caps, raft slab, piles)

Structure (formwork, reinforcement, concrete casting, columns, beams, slabs)

Masonry (partition or structural masonry)

Installations (plumbing, electrical, gas, fire protection)

Finishes and surface treatments

Construction site setup / preliminary services

If there is any uncertainty, explicitly state it.

2. Assessment of Execution Quality

Analyze the quality of what is visible, observing, when applicable:

Alignment, leveling, and verticality

Surface finishing quality

Organization and cleanliness of the work area

Apparent compliance with engineering best practices

Classify the overall perception as adequate, fair, or critical, always providing justification.

3. Identification of Non-Conformities and Risks

Point out possible:

Construction defects

Technical non-conformities

Signs of pathologies (cracks, segregation, exposed reinforcement, moisture, etc.)

Apparent structural risks

Issues that may lead to rework, additional costs, or future risks

If something cannot be confirmed based solely on the image, clearly state this limitation.

4. Occupational Safety and Best Practices

Visually verify:

Use of PPE (helmet, gloves, boots, safety harnesses, etc.)

Shoring, collective protection systems, and temporary stability

Construction site organization

Immediate risks to workers or third parties

Comment in an objective and technical manner.

5. Technical Recommendations

When applicable:

Suggest corrections, adjustments, or preventive measures

Indicate the need for complementary verification (design documents, testing, inspection)

Highlight points that require attention from the technical manager

Response Format

Technical language, yet clear and accessible

Objective text, structured in topics

No speculation beyond what is visible in the image

Suitable for presentation to clients, contractors, or property owners

Mandatory Notice

Begin the analysis as soon as the photo(s) are provided.

parameters.content:
## AI-Driven construction execution monitoring

### How it works

1. A scheduled trigger checks the configured Google Drive input folder for new files.
2. New files are filtered to keep images, downloaded, and sent to the AI model for a technical inspection (identify stage, execution quality, non-conformities, safety issues, and recommendations).
3. The workflow builds a concise, structured report and emails it to the configured recipient with the photo attached.
4. The image is shared via a public link, a log row with timestamp, analysis and image preview is appended to a Google Sheet, and the file is moved to the processed folder.

---

### Setup

- [ ] Connect Google Drive account and set the input folder ID.
- [ ] Connect Gmail account and authorize sending from the workflow.
- [ ] Add OpenAI API key or connect the AI account used by the LangChain/OpenAI node.
- [ ] Set the log spreadsheet ID and sheet name for append rows.
- [ ] Set the processed folder ID where handled images will be moved.
- [ ] Update recipient email and optional subject prefix in the Config node.
- [ ] Configure the schedule interval to run as needed.
## 3. Distribute Report and Log Work Performance Data

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.

data.details: The Filter node can be used to filter items based on a condition. If the condition is met, the item will be passed on to the next node. If the condition is not met, the item will be omitted. Conditions can be combined together by AND(meet all conditions), or OR(meet at least one condition).
